text:
There once was a cat who loved puzzles. Every morning she’d stare out the window. She watched birds hop from branch to branch. She listened rats scuttle under leaves. Finally one day something caught her eye.

There was a large red berry. Only one. And she was determined to have it. She leaped onto the branch and grabbed it. Then she leaped right back off again. The berry bounced away. You’ll never catch me!

She scampered around again. Eventually she stepped right onto a hawk’s head! It screeched loudly pulling her off. She landed awkwardly on the ground—but not hard! Her tail just wagged a little. She scratched the hawk!

So she took a long walk alone. She didn’t hunt—not these hills anyway. Instead, she watched animals. Birds singing beautifully. Frogs croaking. A squirrel nibbled on an acorn. That afternoon she saw a fox.

The fox stopped and stared. For a moment she didn’t
